Article Title: Sonam Wangchuk's wife challenges Delhi HC order, says it 'illegally confines' him to Safdarjung hospital
Article Text: A day after the Delhi high court declined to direct the transfer of climate activist Sonam Wangchuk from Safdarjung Hospital to a private hospital, his wife Dr Gitanjali J Angmo on Monday challenged the order before a Division Bench, alleging that it "illegally confines" him to the government hospital despite him not being under arrest.According to PTI, Angmo argued that "Sunday's order effectively directs that neither Wangchuk nor his wife have decisive authority in determining medical treatment."She further told the court, "Sunday order illegally confines Sonam Wangchuk to Safdarjung hospital without arrest."This came after Wangchuk's wife sought an urgent hearing before the Division Bench challenging the Single Judge's order on Sunday, contending that it violates Wangchuk's fundamental rights to bodily autonomy, informed consent and personal liberty.In her appeal, Angmo argued that the Single Judge's order effectively takes away the authority of Wangchuk and his family to make decisions regarding his medical treatment by leaving the final decision to the doctors at Safdarjung Hospital.The plea states that the order does not adequately address the issue of informed consent and ignores the settled legal principle that a competent patient has the right to accept, refuse or discontinue medical treatment.It further argues that although the Single Judge recorded that Wangchuk is neither under arrest nor detention, the order effectively confines him to Safdarjung Hospital without any legal authority.The appeal alleges that the order authorises medical intervention, including the administration of food, without Wangchuk's consent, thereby violating his bodily autonomy under Article 21 of the Constitution.It also contends that his continued stay at Safdarjung Hospital infringes his rights under Articles 19 and 21, as it prevents him from continuing his peaceful hunger strike and protest at Jantar Mantar.According to the appeal, Wangchuk was never consulted before being shifted to Safdarjung Hospital, and neither he nor his wife was given the opportunity to choose the hospital or the doctors treating him.The plea further argues that there was no evidence of any deterioration in his health warranting such coercive action. It says that if the authorities believed immediate medical intervention was necessary, they should have sought the court's permission before removing him from the protest site.The appeal seeks to set aside the July 19 order and requests directions allowing Wangchuk to be shifted to a private hospital of his choice while safeguarding his rights relating to medical treatment and personal liberty.On Saturday, Justice Mini Pushkarna refused to grant interim relief, observing that there was no prima facie violation of Wangchuk's personal liberty, as the government's decision to shift him from the protest site was taken to protect his life after his health deteriorated during a 17-18 day fast.The court noted that the transfer to Safdarjung Hospital was carried out in compliance with a Division Bench order dated July 16 directing daily medical monitoring and necessary medical intervention to prevent further deterioration in his condition.The High Court also observed that doctors were administering only those medicines and oral electrolytes to which Wangchuk had consented and found nothing to suggest that force was being used against him or that his bodily autonomy had been violated.It further recorded the Centre's submission that Wangchuk's wife, brother and brother-in-law had been granted unrestricted 24x7 access to meet him and that a separate room had been provided for the family. The court also noted the Centre's assurance that Wangchuk's medical reports would be shared with his family.Holding that no case for interim relief was made out, the high court directed the Union government to file a status report within three days and listed the matter for further hearing on July 24.
Article Summary: Sonam wangchuk's wife dr gitanjali j angmo on monday challenged the order before a division bench. She alleges that it "illegally confines" him to the government hospital despite him not being under arrest. On saturday, the delhi high court declined to direct the transfer of wangchuk from safdarjung hospital to a private hospital.

Article Title: Wangchuk's wife moves Delhi HC again over plea to shift him to private hospital
Article Text:
Wangchuk wife's appeal claims that his continued stay at Safdarjung Hospital violates his fundamental rights.

NEW DELHI: Gitanjali Angmo, activist Sonam Wangchuk ’s wife, Monday moved the Delhi high court again, challenging single bench order that declined her plea to shift Wangchuk from Safdarjung Hospital to a private hospital of his choice.In the appeal, Angmo has contended that the Single Judge’s order effectively deprives Wangchuk and his family of the right to decide his medical treatment and place of treatment. The appeal also argues that the order overlooks the principles of informed consent, bodily autonomy under Article 21, and unlawfully confines Wangchuk to the government hospital despite there being no order of arrest or detention against him, reported news agency ANI.The appeal further claims that Wangchuk’s continued stay at Safdarjung Hospital violates his fundamental rights under Articles 19 and 21, including his right to continue his protest , and relies on Supreme Court judgments on informed consent and patients’ autonomy.According to the legal team, senior counsel will mention the appeal before the Division Bench today, seeking urgent listing and hearing.Meanwhile, Wangchuk said he would end his indefinite hunger strike on July 20 if the Centre takes responsibility for alleged failures in the education system, or if political leaders assure him that the issues will be raised in Parliament.In a message posted on X from Delhi’s Safdarjung Hospital, where he has been admitted after being shifted from the Jantar Mantar protest site, Wangchuk listed three conditions under which he would call off his fast. He described the hospital stay as an “illegal detention”."I will end my fast on 20th July if: a. If the govt takes accountability of the recent failures in Education system, Paper leaks etc. If I and the leadership of CJP reach the doorsteps of Parliament where MPs and leaders of various parties assure us that they will now take up the issue in the Parliament," he wrote.The letter further read: "If my health or other factors do not permit this then the MPs and leaders of different parties visit this Hospital and give give the the above assurance ...From the illegal detentions at Safdarjung Hospital, where my freedom of movement, speech and communication are restricted."While the battle on the legal front continues, the Cockroach Janata Party members gear up for the proposed 'Chalo Sansad' march to Parliament despite Delhi Police denying permission for the rally.Security personnel were deployed outside Jantar Mantar and around Parliament house ahead of the first day of the Monsoon Session. Police personnel maintained a vigil at the protest site amid expectations of large crowds.
Article Summary: Wangchuk wife's appeal claims that his continued stay at safdarjung hospital violates his fundamental rights. Wangchuk said he would end his indefinite hunger strike on july 20 if the centre takes responsibility for alleged failures in the education system.

Article Title: 'I'm starting an indefinite hunger strike': CJP's Abhijeet Dipke announces fast after Sonam Wangchuk removed from Jantar Mantar
Article Text:
Dipke announces hunger strike (PTI image)

NEW DELHI:

Demands PM's resignation

Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke on Saturday announced that he has begun an indefinite hunger strike, saying the fast starts "right now" after activist Sonam Wangchuk was removed from the protest site at Jantar Mantar earlier in the day.Announcing the decision on X, Dipke wrote, "I am starting an indefinite hunger strike beginning right now," marking the next phase of the group's agitation."I appeal to everyone: do not back down; this movement will only grow larger. They have made a grave mistake... I urge everyone to come to Jantar Mantar; our movement will continue from here, and we will proceed with our march scheduled for July 20th," he said while addressing the protestors.Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike against alleged irregularities in the NEET examination entered Day 21 on Saturday. He was removed from the stage 48 hours before his planned march to Parliament, and has been taken to VMMC & Safdarjung Hospital.CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke on Saturday also claimed that he was "beaten and put under detention" by the Delhi Police as chaos erupted at Jantar Mantar after Sonam Wangchuk was taken to hospital.Escalating his demands, he called for Prime Minister Narendra Modi's resignation."If they think that taking Sonam sir away will end this movement, they are mistaken. We will remain here and march to Parliament on July 20," Dipke said."Until now, we were demanding Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. But after this despicable act, we will now demand Narendra Modi's resignation," he added.CJP has been spearheading protest at Jantar Mantar in New Delhi since June 20, demanding accountability over the alleged examination irregularities and seeking the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Wangchuk joined the agitation on June 28 and was remain on an indefinite hunger strike since then.As per CJP's official X account annoucement, the ‘Chalo Sansad’ march on July 20 will proceed as planned.
Article Summary: Cjp founder abhijeet dipke says fast starts "right now" activist sonam wangchuk was removed from protest site at jantar mantar earlier in the day. Wangchuk's hunger strike against alleged irregularities in the neet examination entered day 21 on saturday.

Article Title: 'There was no need to bring him to hospital', says Sonam Wangchuk's wife; CJP attacks Modi govt - top developments
Article Text:
Activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on an indefinite hunger strike for 21 days, waves as he is shifted to a hospital from Jantar Mantar.

Sonam Wangchuk was on an indefinite hunger strike for 21 days.

CJP protest timeline

'What a reign of thugs ...': Opposition attacks Centre

'I'm starting an indefinite hunger strike': Dipke

Stable but requires continuous monitoring: Safdarjung Hospital

'No treatment should start without my consent: Wangchuk's wife

CJP targets PM Modi

What Delhi Police said

Dipke claims Delhi Police beat him up

Action amid Wangchuk's health concerns

Detained before planned march

ALSO READ |

NEW DELHI: Activist Sonam Wangchuk was removed from Jantar Mantar early Saturday and taken to VMMC & Safdarjung Hospital as his hunger strike against alleged irregularities in the NEET examination entered Day 21.Wangchuk was removed from the stage 48 hours before his planned march to Parliament, which is scheduled for the first day of the Monsoon Session on Monday.Amid heavy security, police personnel in plain clothes entered the protest site, climbed onto the stage, draped Wangchuk and the entire scene with a white sheet to prevent the operation from being captured on camera.The entire operation lasted just a few minutes.Simultaneously, Cockroach Janta Party ( CJP ) founder Abhijeet Dipke also claimed that he was detained Opposition leaders across party lines on Saturday condemned the Delhi police action at Jantar Mantar after activist Sonam Wangchuk was taken to Safdarjung Hospital during his indefinite hunger strike.The leaders alleged that police used force against peaceful protesters, accusing the centre of suppressing dissent and questioning its response to Wangchuk’s demands over the NEET-UG paper leak.LoP Rahul Gandhi called his removal from Jantar Mantar 'wrong' and accusing the Centre of using force against a peaceful protest.Read more: Rahul Gandhi slams BJP's 'asatya and hinsa' approach in first reaction to Jantar Mantar protest Meanwhile, AAP Rajya Sabha MP Sanjay Singh posted on X."What kind of thuggery is this going on? Modi ji, this arrogance of power doesn't last long. The very youth you're beating with batons will uproot your throne. One person @Wangchuk66 who has been on a fast unto death for the past 21 days, instead of listening to his demands, was forcibly arrested and admitted to the hospital."Trinamool Congress leader Sagarika Ghose also criticised the police action. "What sort of shocking coercive state violence is this? The morally bankrupt @narendramodi regime only knows how to use the danda. UNACCEPTABLE," she posted on X.NCP (SP) chief Sharad Pawar also criticised the government's handling of Wangchuk's protest, alleging it ignored students' concerns and acted only after the situation became "uncontrollable".Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke on Saturday announced that he has begun an indefinite hunger strike , saying the fast starts "right now" after activist Sonam Wangchuk was removed from the protest site at Jantar Mantar earlier in the day.Announcing the decision on X, Dipke wrote, "I am starting an indefinite hunger strike beginning right now," marking the next phase of the group's agitation.Later in the day, Dipke claimed that Delhi police could shut down thewhole protest at Jantar Mantar while urging everyone to show up. "Delhi Police is planning to crackdown on our protest during night or early hours of morning - silencing the voice of youth. We request everyone to join us for night vigil at Jantar Mantar, so that together we can march to Sansad on 20th," he said.Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk remains stable but requires continuous observation, monitoring and treatment to restore his body parameters.According to Safdarjung Hospital, Wangchuk is "weak due to prolonged fasting and dehydration"."Mr Sonam Wangchuck has been admitted to Safdarjung Hospital for the required health care. He is weak due to prolonged fasting and dehydration. Although he is currently stable, he requires continuous observation, monitoring and treatment to restore his body parameters," the Safdarjung Hospital said.According to the statement, Wangchuk was admitted with complaints of generalised weakness. He had no history of fainting episodes and was conscious at the time of admission, with stable pulse, blood pressure and oxygen saturation. However, doctors observed signs of dehydration.Meanwhile, Wangchuk's personal physician, Dr Nitin Dighe, questioned the hospital's assessment of the activist's condition and alleged a lack of transparency in his medical care.Read more: Sonam Wangchuk's doctor rejects Safdurjung report claiming potassium deficiency Wangchuk's wife Gitanjali J Angmo said that no medical treatment should be given to Wangchuk without her consent."He is at Safdarjung Hospital. I forbid them from administering anything without my consent. No treatment should start without my consent," Angmo told news agency PTI."I hold everybody accountable if anything happens," she added.Questioning the decision to hospitalise him, she claimed Wangchuk appeared fine on Friday."Yesterday, he was doing fine. There was no need to bring him to the hospital. It's my right under Article 32. Without my consent and my doctor's consent, nothing can be administered," she said.The Delhi Police has said that Wangchuk was shifted to the hospital for "essential medical care" following expert medical advice and in compliance with the Delhi high court's orders.Wangchuk's contested the Delhi Police's arugument, saying the court's order never mandated hospitalisation and that the high court "simply stated that an individual's health is paramount and must be monitored at regular intervals"."This is not in accordance with the high court order. No treatment is underway right now. Only observation and tests are being conducted, and we are actually going to get the tests done at an external lab. Since they aren't providing the reports we requested, and regarding the figures they are citing like the potassium level reaching 2.9. It was 4.3 yesterday, so it couldn't have changed to that today," she futher said.Gitanjali J Angmo said that there was "no need for government interference in this matter", admitting that Wangchuk is "definitely weak and losing muscle mass"."We want to cross-check this with another lab before administering any medication. He is still fasting; the fast is continuing because he isn't consuming any sugar. He is only taking the water with salt that he was consuming earlier... I am grateful that the government cared enough to bring him here, but we will handle the next steps ourselves; there is no need for government interference in this matter. He is definitely weak and losing muscle mass—which happens during any fast—but he is alert and very strong," Gitanjali J Angmo said.CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke on Saturday escalated his demands, calling for Prime Minister Narendra Modi's resignation after Sonam Wangchuk was taken away from the protest site."If they think that taking Sonam sir away will end this movement, they are mistaken. We will remain here and march to Parliament on July 20," Dipke said."Until now, we were demanding Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. But after this despicable act, we will now demand Narendra Modi's resignation," he added.Delhi Police said the Ladakh activist was removed as per the orders of high court and on expert medical advise due to his deteriorating health condition "He has been shifted to the hospital for essential medical care...We request the protestors at Jantar Mantar to peacefully vacate the place at the earliest," DCP New Delhi posted on X.The police also urged the protestors at the site to "peacefully vacate the place at the earliest", saying the cops faced obstruction while "complying with the orders" of the high court."While complying with the orders of Hon’ble High Court the protestors tried to create obstruction, in which slight commotion ensued, however police took maximum restrain and undertook the exercise safely. We request the protestors at Jantar Mantar to peacefully vacate the place at the earliest," the Delhi Police wrote on X.CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke on Saturday claimed that he was "beaten and put under detention" by the Delhi Police as chaos erupted at Jantar Mantar after Sonam Wangchuk was taken to hospital.In a social media post on X, Dipke alleged that the Delhi Police was cracking down at Jantar Mantar and beating people.Earlier, Dipke alleged that "goons tried to attack" activist Sonam Wangchuk at Jantar Mantar amid his ongoing hunger strike. In a social media post on X, Dipke said that an object was thrown at Wangchuk, but he was not hurt."Goons tried to attack Sonam sir at Jantar Mantar. An object was thrown at him, but fortunately, he was not hurt," Dipke said.Meanwhile, brief chaos erupted when a woman threw ink at the CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke as he was addressing the gathering at Jantar Mantar. The incident briefly disrupted the programme as supporters rushed towards the stage, triggering a commotion.Read more: Woman hurls ink at CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke as protest continues at Jantar Mantar According to Wangchuk's medical team, as of 9.30 am on Friday, he showed signs of mild dehydration and weighed 56.55 kg, having lost 350 grams in the previous 24 hours as he entered Day 20 of the fast.His blood pressure was 108/68 mmHg, blood sugar 70 mg/dL and pulse 72 per minute. Doctors said he had entered the third stage of prolonged fasting, where organ involvement is possible, and remained under round-the-clock medical monitoring.Sonam Wangchuk lost more than 9kgs since beginning his indefinite hunger strike, with doctors warning that he may soon enter a potentially "alarming" phase involving organ damage.On the Day 19 of his fast, Dr Satish Lamba, providing an update on Wangchuk's health, said the activist remained mentally alert and medically stable, but warned that doctors were closely monitoring his condition in case it deteriorated.In a video message shared at the end of the Day 20 of his fast on Friday, Wangchuk urged people to join the CJP Parliament march on July 20 in large numbers, saying public participation was the movement's biggest strength."Yes, I am still alive. Twenty per cent of my body is gone. After fats, muscles are gone. After that, organs will go. Finally, the brain. The time has not come yet," Wangchuk said.Seeking to reassure supporters about his condition, he added, "The 20th day is coming to an end. Let me prove that my mind is still fine."Referring to questions over whether the ongoing agitation would lead to accountability or the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, Wangchuk said people often underestimated the power of mass movements."I ask you -- do the people of India love their children's lives and education more, or onions?" he said, before recalling that governments had fallen in the past over public anger triggered by soaring onion prices."Three times governments fell in India because of a people's movement. Once in 1980, the central government fell. In 1998, the Delhi government fell. That year, the Rajasthan government also fell. And what was the movement about? Onion prices," he said.The CJP was holding a protest at Jantar Mantar since June 20, demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged irregularities in the NEET examination, a judicial probe into the alleged examination scams and wider reforms in the examination system.Wangchuk joined the agitation on June 28 and was remain on an indefinite hunger strike since then.
Article Summary: Activist sonam wangchuk has been on an indefinite hunger strike for 21 days. He was removed from the stage 48 hours before his planned march to parliament. Opposition leaders across party lines condemned the delhi police action at jantar mantar. Wangchuk's wife gitanjali j angmo said no medical treatment should be given to him without her consent.

Article Title: Sonam Wangchuk health update: Doctors warn of possible organ impact after formation of ketone bodies in urine
Article Text:
(With agency inputs)





NEW DELHI: Educator and climate activist Sonam Wangchuk on Friday said he would "stay alive till July 20 at any cost" as his indefinite hunger strike entered its 20th day, even as doctors warned that his prolonged fast had reached a critical stage.Meanwhile, Wangchuk's health condition showed signs of concern on the 20th day of his hunger strike.Dr Satish Lamba said his health parameters showed that his weight had dropped to 56.55 kg, a reduction of 350 grams over the previous 24 hours."It is the 20th day of the hunger strike. As of 9:30 am on 17 July 2026, the key health parameters are as follows. The person's weight is 56.55 kg, a reduction of 350 grams over the last 24 hours. Blood pressure is 108/68, blood sugar is 70 mg/dL, and the pulse rate is 72 per minute. Hydration is fair, with mild dehydration observed, " Dr Satish Lamba said.Dr Lamba said that prolonged fasting causes changes in the body as it runs out of glucose, proteins and carbohydrates. He explained that the initial stage involves fat loss, followed by muscle loss and the formation of ketone bodies in urine.At the current stage, he said, organ involvement is possible, and the medical team was maintaining 24-hour vigilance and continuous monitoring."As explained earlier, when the body does not receive glucose, protein and carbohydrates, changes occur. The initial stage involved fat loss, followed by muscle loss and the formation of ketone bodies in urine. Now, at this third stage, organ involvement is possible. The medical team is maintaining 24-hour vigilance and continuous monitoring, " Dr Satish Lamba added.The Cockroach Janta Party's (CJP) protest at Jantar Mantar over alleged examination irregularities entered its 28th day, with Wangchuk urging people to participate in large numbers in the outfit's proposed march to Parliament on July 20.Addressing supporters at the protest site, Wangchuk acknowledged that his physical condition was deteriorating but said his determination to continue the fight remained unchanged."I am weak from the outside but very strong inside. I am sure all of you are strong from the inside, and outside too. We need this energy for July 20, when we will take out a peaceful march to Parliament. We will go together and present our plea in the temple of democracy," he said."I will stay alive till July 20 at any cost. If you don't come and July 20 is not successful, I will come back as a ghost," he said in a lighter vein, drawing cheers from the gathering.Meanwhile, senior Congress leader Pawan Khera on Friday met activist Sonam Wangchuk.Khera expressed concern over Wangchuk's health and extended support for his demand for greater accountability and transparency in examinations.Khera, who was among the opposition leaders who met Wangchuk, criticised the government's handling of protests and alleged that it was "insensitive" towards students' concerns."We are all concerned about Sonam Wangchuk's health. We are dealing with a highly insensitive government that fails to understand the language of democratic protest. When facing such a government, the methods of protest must evolve... Risking one's life against this government will not yield results," Khera said.The CJP has been demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan and Rs 1 crore compensation for the families of students who allegedly died by suicide amid the examination irregularities row. The outfit has announced a peaceful march to Parliament on July 20, coinciding with the first day of the Monsoon Session.The CJP's protest began on June 20, while Wangchuk joined the agitation on June 28 and has been on an indefinite hunger strike since then.
Article Summary: Sonam wangchuk's health condition showed signs of concern on the 20th day of his hunger strike. Dr satish lamba said that prolonged fasting causes changes in the body as it runs out of glucose, proteins and carbohydrates. He explained that the initial stage involves fat loss, followed by muscle loss and the formation of ketone bodies in urine.

Article Title: Minister Manohar Lal dismantles Central Public Works Department’s ‘Delhi Club’
Article Text:
Manohar Lal Khattar. (File Photo)

NEW DELHI: For a long time, a group of officials belonging to Central Public Works Department (CPWD) have attracted both envy and admiration for something they seem to possess in abundance — their tenacious rootedness while holding transferable jobs.Housing and urban affairs minister Manohar Lal, however, revealed the secret of how they transformed into permanent fixtures, saying, “Ever since we rolled out a transparent transfer policy, I have been approached by many, including some influential people, to get the transfers rescinded.”Speaking at CPWD’s foundation day celebrations recently, the minister said, “I was told there were some people who never got transferred for 20-30 years. No one was able to move them out. Big and influential people started approaching me for them when transfer orders were issued, saying they have known such employees for years. I told them that it would happen this time and we would see next year.”Lal was referring to one of the most commonly known secrets of the city — the knack of CPWD employees in winning over Lutyens’ influentials who become dependent on them for their comforts, resulting in an equilibrium of convenience that few would like disrupted.The minister said employees should think of what would benefit all their colleagues and not just themselves. He also urged CPWD staff to put in their best efforts to achieve public good.Lal said as CM of Haryana, he had introduced a transfer policy for teachers in 2015 . He recalled that during former CM Chaudhary Bansi Lal’s tenure, when all ministers had offices on the fourth floor of the state secretariat, the education minister’s room would be full during “transfer season”.“I had suggested to the then CM and the education minister in 1996-97 that they should have a transfer policy. Some people paid heed to my suggestion, but said if it was done, people would be unhappy. They said no one would approach influential people with requests for transfers. Aur agar koi political aadmi ke dwar par koi aadmi nehin aayega toh usko roti kaise hazam hogi (If none will approach political leaders for transfers, how would they survive),” he said.Lal said after the introduction of the transfer policy, people are happy as they don’t depend on anyone’s mercy. The transfers were done in accordance with criteria that had been laid down and a survey showed 93% of teachers were satisfied, the minister added.
Article Summary: Housing and urban affairs minister manohar lal revealed the secret of how they transformed into permanent fixtures. “ever since we rolled out a transparent transfer policy, i have been approached by many, including some influential people, to get the transfers rescinded,” he said.

Article Title: Monsoon session begins today as oppn gears up to corner Centre over temple row, NEET, E20
Article Text:
Kiren Rijiju called for consultation instead of a “my way or highway” attitude over the issues to be picked up for debate.

Govt lists 8-bill agenda, silent on delimitation, women's quota





NEW DELHI: The stage is set for a stormy Monsoon Session of Parliament from today, with the opposition set to raise several key issues. On Sunday, opposition leaders pushed for discussions on the alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple, the NEET paper leak, the CJP protest at Jantar Mantar, and the E20 petrol row. The government, meanwhile, has cautioned its rivals that any disruption of the legislature is bound to cost them politically.Amid indications that govt is unlikely to allow its rivals to raise in Parliament the theft of temple donations, parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju called for consultation instead of a “my way or highway” attitude over the issues to be picked up for debate.“Congress has every right to raise its demand, but it can’t force govt,” he said, after Rahul Gandhi and Mallikarjun Kharge wrote to PM Modi, timing their demand for probe and accountability with the session’s start, as several opposition MPs, including SP’s Dharmendra Yadav, raised the issue in an all-party meet led by defence minister Rajnath Singh and attended by 58 MPs of 40 parties.While listing its agenda of eight bills, govt made no mention of its likely move to revive the constitution amendment bill for raising the strength of Lok Sabha and rolling out one-third reservation for women from 2029.Rijiju, though, told reporters later that govt could bring a new item in accordance with the rules after discussing it in the business advisory committee (BAC) during the session, a common practice.He said people had seen that political parties that disrupt Parliament and do not allow discussion end up paying a price, an apparent reminder to BJP's rivals of their poor show in a string of assembly polls, with once-formidable regional parties Trinamool Congress and DMK losing in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu, respectively.With several opposition members raising the NEET-UG paper leak and questioning govt for not engaging with protesters at Jantar Mantar, RSP MP N K Premachandran pushed for a structured debate over the issue, saying paper leaks affect students in huge numbers.As some opposition MPs spoke of the recent fuel price hike, BJP ally and JDU MP Sanjay Jha sought a debate on the West Asia crisis as he credited govt for its energy management, saying it had ensured minimal rise in oil and gas prices compared with other countries and expanded supply for India through alternative sources. India had no role in the crisis, but it handled its adverse impacts deftly, he said.At a BAC meeting later, Speaker Om Birla appealed to all parties for cooperation in ensuring smooth and orderly conduct of the proceedings.
Article Summary: Kiren rijiju called for consultation instead of a ‘my way or highway’ attitude over the issues to be picked up for debate. Opposition leaders pushed for discussions on alleged embezzlement of donations at the ram temple, the neet paper leak, the cjp protest at jantar mantar and the e20 petrol row.

Article Title: Well-oiled labour supply chain facilitating illegal infiltration of Bangladeshis
Article Text: NEW DELHI: A large majority of Bangladeshi illegal immigrants who sneak into India are not here to organically ‘explore’ economic opportunities; they cross over as part of a well-oiled labour supply chain, with Indian employers in states far from the border relying on headhunting touts in Bangladesh for cheap labour.The ‘jobs for illegal immigrants’ eco-system has touts at every stage — facilitating infiltration, transportation to job sites and arranging forged documentation before settling them into jobs across sectors such as textile manufacturing, construction and pharmaceuticals.“It’s an organised eco-system that comprises Bangladeshi recruitment touts who, based on requirements of Indian employers for cheap labour, identify unemployed Bangladeshis seeking low-paid, unskilled jobs in India. Every step, from their illegal entry into India to reaching their job locations, is planned well in advance,” a senior Assam govt official told TOI.The official said infiltration guides help the selected Bangladeshis cross over, mostly through Meghalaya, Tripura and West Bengal. Agents on the Indian side arrange their transit to Assam and onward train journeys from Guwahati to job locations across the country.Local touts help them secure forged documents like Aadhaar before another set of agents hand them over to Indian employers willing to hire them at wages lower than those payable to Indians.Assam, which recently busted one such tout network transporting illegal immigrants from Guwahati railway station to Tamil Nadu to work in a textile factory, leading to 34 arrests, raised the issue at the all-India conference of SPs of border districts chaired by home minister Amit Shah on July 9. The state sought a legal framework to hold Indian employers exploiting the Bangladeshi illegal immigration network accountable.Intelligence sources said Bangladeshi headhunters work on labour demand conveyed by agents linked to Indian employers. The network also arranges forged Aadhaar and PAN cards in destination states, enabling illegal immigrants to register as workers and move between work sites.“Armed with the same documents, many went on to project themselves as Indian citizens and even enrolled as voters. Special intensive revision (SIR), however, may have cut their dream run short by requiring proof of their parents’ or relatives’ presence in the 2002 rolls,” a police officer told TOI.
Article Summary: The ‘jobs for illegal immigrants’ eco-system has touts at every stage. Touts facilitate infiltration, transportation to job sites and arranging forged documentation. Indian employers in states far from the border rely on headhunting touts in bangladesh for cheap labour.

Article Title: 'Stay here for the night': CJP's Abhijeet Dipke calls for night-long vigil ahead of 'Chalo Sansad' march
Article Text:
Abhijeet Dipke is currently on a hunger strike at Jantar Mantar (Image/PTI)

NEW DELHI: Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) founder Abhijeet Dipke on Sunday called on supporters to hold a night-long vigil at Jantar Mantar ahead of the outfit's proposed 'Chalo Sansad' march to Parliament on Monday.Meanwhile, activist Sonam Wangchuk 's wife said he would end his hunger strike if political leaders assured him that the issue of education accountability would be raised during the Monsoon Session of Parliament.The appeal came as Jantar Mantar witnessed a surge in the number of protesters a day before the Monsoon Session of Parliament, with supporters from different parts of the country gathering at the protest site.Addressing supporters through a video message, Dipke urged them to remain at the protest site through the night to ensure the planned march proceeded peacefully."Our protest is going on. Please come here in large numbers. Stay here for the night. If you don't come, they will try to end the protest," he said.Appealing for restraint, Dipke added, "Our protest and march will be peaceful. I request everyone to follow the rules and follow the guidelines."He also said Wangchuk's indefinite hunger strike was continuing from Safdarjung Hospital despite his removal from the protest site by police on Saturday."As you saw, they took away Sonam sir forcefully, but his hunger strike is continuing in the hospital," Dipke said.According to CJP chief spokesperson Saurav Das, nearly 20,000 people were 'at and around Jantar Mantar' even before the scheduled mobilisation.In a post on X, Das said, "20,000 people are at and around Jantar Mantar. And it's not even July 20 yet. Dharmendra Pradhan -- your day of reckoning is here."The protest also received support from several political leaders and public figures. Actors Shabana Azmi and Prakash Raj visited Jantar Mantar during the day, joining Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra, CPI(M) leader Brinda Karat and AAP leader Manish Sisodia in expressing solidarity with the agitation.In a significant announcement, Wangchuk's wife, Gitanjali J Angmo, told supporters that the activist was prepared to end his fast if political leaders visited him in hospital and assured him that the issue of education accountability would be raised during the Monsoon Session."If political leaders meet Sonam in the hospital and assure him that they will raise the issue of education accountability during the Parliament session, he will end his hunger strike tomorrow," Angmo said.She also conveyed Wangchuk's message to supporters ahead of Monday's march."Sonam has requested all of us to take tomorrow's march peacefully and ensure that this protest is not misused," she said.Angmo further alleged that the family had lost faith in Safdarjung Hospital and had approached the Delhi high court seeking permission to shift Wangchuk to a private hospital. She claimed the hospital had neither discharged him nor allowed the transfer despite repeated requests and alleged that he was effectively under 'illegal detention' because of the heavy police presence.Earlier in the day, Wangchuk issued a message from Safdarjung Hospital through Angmo, describing Monday's mobilisation as 'India's second freedom movement' and calling for 'freedom from injustice' over the alleged NEET paper leak and 'freedom from fear' over what he termed his 'illegal detention'. He appealed to people to make the Parliament march a success.The CJP also expressed concern over possible police action ahead of the march. In posts on X, the outfit urged supporters to reach Jantar Mantar immediately, claimed tear-gas equipment had been brought to the area and alleged that anti-social elements could attempt to infiltrate the gathering to provoke disturbances. It appealed to protesters not to respond to any provocation and to keep the march peaceful.Following Wangchuk's removal from the protest site early on Saturday, Dipke began his own indefinite hunger strike. Entering its second day on Sunday, he said he had now understood the physical toll of a prolonged fast and appealed to people to strengthen the movement by joining Monday's Parliament march in large numbers.The CJP has been protesting at Jantar Mantar since June 20, demanding the resignation of union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged irregularities in the NEET examination, reforms in the examination system and justice for affected students.Meanwhile, Delhi Police has clarified that no permission has been sought or granted for the proposed march to Parliament. It said prohibitory orders under Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) are in force in the New Delhi district, prohibiting protest marches, processions and assemblies of five or more people except at the designated protest site at Jantar Mantar with prior permission.The police said strict security arrangements have been put in place for the Monsoon Session of Parliament and warned that anyone violating the prohibitory orders would face action under Section 223 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) and other applicable provisions of law.It also appealed to citizens to refrain from participating in any unauthorised gathering or march and cooperate in maintaining public peace and security.
Article Summary: Cockroach janta party (cjp) founder abhijeet dipke on sunday called on supporters to hold a night-long vigil at jantar mantar ahead of the outfit's proposed 'chalo sansad' march to parliament on monday. Activist sonam wangchuk's wife said he would end his hunger strike if political leaders assured him that the issue of education accountability would be raised during the monsoon session of parliament.

Article Title: Net services remain suspended in J&K’s Doda 3 days after man’s death in police firing
Article Text: JAMMU: Three days after a 30-year-old man was shot dead in police firing, triggering protests by locals, internet services continued to remain suspended across Doda district of J&K for the third consecutive day on Sunday, disrupting communication, online transactions and other services.Officials have not yet issued any statement regarding the restoration of internet services.Locals complained that students, remote workers and job seekers are struggling with the internet shutdown, besides tourists as they can’t make online payments through apps.Sources claimed the internet speed on mobile and the broadband as well has also been restricted in Kishtwar amid the heightened security in the region.On July 16, a police team attempted to stop suspicious individuals in the Jai Valley area on suspicions of cattle smuggling. During the interception, a scuffle broke out and there was an alleged attempt to snatch an officer’s weapon, following which another member of the patrol party opened fire, killing 30-year-old Arif Hussain and injuring three other SOG personnel.Following the death of Arif, an autorickshaw driver, protests erupted in Bhaderwah town of Doda district and surrounding areas.According to police, initial probe has revealed no terrorist links.
Article Summary: A 30-year-old man was shot dead in police firing, triggering protests by locals. On july 16, a police team attempted to stop suspicious individuals in the jai valley area. During the interception, a scuffle broke out and there was an alleged attempt to snatch an officer’s weapon.

Article Title: DMK confirms TOI report it’s open to rethink on delimit bill
Article Text:
Lok Sabha (File photo)

NEW DELHI: Let govt bring the bill on raising the strength of Lok Sabha, then DMK will spell out its stand, the regional party with a crucial bloc of 22 MPs in the House said Sunday, confirming that the party is willing to revisit its stand on delimitation if its provisions do not harm the interest of southern states.Senior DMK functionary Tiruchi Siva said govt should first make it clear as to what is on its mind as his party wants more clarity on the matter. TOI had exclusively reported Saturday that DMK is open to negotiation with govt if it is given an ironclad guarantee that the interests of southern states will not be adversely affected.“I want to underscore if southern states are affected, then we will not accept it in any manner. Our representation in Parliament should remain...,” he said in what appeared to be his pitch for maintaining the existing share of strength of southern states. As part of INDIA bloc in the last Parliament session, DMK was at the forefront of protest against the constitution amendment bill, which was eventually defeated as a joint opposition denied a two-thirds majority govt needed for its passage.DMK is no longer part of INDIA bloc as its ally Congress joined hands with actor Vijay-led TVK, which trounced the M K Stalin-led party in the Tamil Nadu assembly polls, giving rise to the possibility that it may be open to business with BJP on renegotiated terms.Asked about Shah’s assurance, Siva said, “He (Shah) had said he will bring a bill. Let the bill come out and we will spell out DMK’s stand.”To a question about his stand if the seats are raised by 50% in all states, he said he will not react to speculation.
Article Summary: Dmk is open to negotiation with govt if it is given an ironclad guarantee that the interests of southern states will not be adversely affected. To a question about his stand if the seats are raised by 50% in all states, he said he will not react to speculation.

Article Title: Opposition protests invite to TMC rebels to all-party meeting
Article Text: NEW DELHI: In solidarity with the Mamata Banerjee-headed Trinamool Congress, opposition parties on Sunday staged a rare walkout from the all-party meeting to protest the govt’s invite to its breakaway faction, even as both warring groups took potshots at one another, foretelling a fiery session of Parliament from Monday.Govt, however, defended its decision, asserting that it cannot deny space to the faction of 20 MPs who have approached Speaker Om Birla for recognising their merger with Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI) and have already been allotted separate seats in Lok Sabha.“It is govt’s duty to invite every group,” parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju told reporters. Playing down the walkout, he said it was only for a brief period as opposition parties rejoined the meeting soon.Facing disintegration and an existential crisis, TMC has leaned on other opposition parties, including Congress, which used to get short shrift from the party in Parliament when Mamata was West Bengal’s chief minister, to strengthen its cause.John Brittas of CPM, which has been a bitter critic of Mamata, said they supported the party in the walkout in the interests of parliamentary democracy which is under “attack” from BJP.As Mahua Moitra, who is among the eight Lok Sabha MPs siding with Mamata, called the majority bloc of 20 MPs “traitors” and demanded their disqualification, Sudip Bandyopadhyay, the chosen leader of breakaway faction, retorted that a “small” party with only eight MPs no longer deserves front row seating in House.Mahua was joined by members of other opposition parties as she slammed the invitation to Sudip as the NCPI leader and to Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar as its chief whip and claimed that they cannot be recognised under anti-defection law. Only original party can merge with another party, she added.
Article Summary: Opposition parties staged a rare walkout from the all-party meeting to protest the govt’s invite to its breakaway faction. Govt says it cannot deny space to the faction of 20 mps who have approached speaker om birla for recognising their merger.

Article Title: 'Fabrications aimed at making bad performance': NTA warns of criminal action on AI, fake OMRs
Article Text:
‘Fabrications Aimed At Masking Bad Performance’

NEW DELHI: Acting swiftly on a wave of post-result complaints, National Testing Agency (NTA) has warned of strict action against fake claims after finding that several optical mark recognition (OMR) sheets submitted in support of NEET-UG result grievances were fake or AI-generated - the first time the agency detecting AI-made OMRs being used to challenge results.The complaints relate to the June 21 NEET-UG retest, held after the May test was cancelled over paper leak. NTA on Sunday warned that candidates submitting digitally-altered OMRs or scorecards could face cancellation of results, debarment from NTA-led exams and even criminal proceedings.Some candidates and parents - few even joining Cockroach Janta Party's Jantar Mantar protest - have alleged mismatches between expected and declared scores. The allegations, spreading rapidly on social media, are also being quickly picked up by opposition parties to attack NTA, which has already faced flak over the paper leak.Faced with the challenge - which marks a technological twist to earlier disputes that involved forged documents, purportedly torn or damaged answer sheets, and allegedly fabricated scorecards - NTA warned candidates submitting digitally altered OMRs or scorecards could face action.Officials said the fabrications appeared aimed at masking "not-so-good performances" - even though the effort cannot fetch a seat in a medical college, as admissions are made by the Medical Counselling Committee under Directorate General of Health Services and the respective state counselling authorities, on the basis of the merit list issued by NTA."NTA is closely monitoring and scrutinising all complaints. In the wake of many OMR sheets submitted for scrutiny turning out to be fake/AI generated, students and parents are advised to submit only original OMRs for scrutiny. Any fake/AI generated OMR may invite legal action against the complainant," the agency said Saturday. Sources in the agency were furious over fabrications saying that they might taint those who have succeeded because of sheer merit.Officials said complaints verified so far had not established any discrepancy in declared results. "If any discrepancy is found during the ongoing verification, corrective measures will be taken. The total number and nature of such cases will be shared once the exercise is completed and all grievances have been addressed," an official said. The disclosure will cover the number and nature of complaints examined, the cases in which documents were fabricated and whether any declared result required correction, they said.
Article Summary: Some candidates and parents have alleged mismatches between expected and declared scores. The complaints relate to the june 21 neet-ug retest, held after the may test was cancelled over paper leak. Nta on sunday warned that candidates submitting digitally-altered omrs or scorecards could face action.

Article Title: Congress demands independent probe into donation chori
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Leaders of opposition Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi Sunday shot off a letter to PM Narendra Modi demanding an independent probe into the Ram temple donation issue and warning that his govt's credibility depends on its swift action - putting the controversy high on the opposition's agenda for Parliament's monsoon session that may lead to confrontation with govt.Also, with Cockroach Janta Party and activist Sonam Wangchuk planned to march to Parliament Monday over paper leaks and to demand the resignation of the education minister, many opposition parties have supported the protest, and are likely to turn up at Jantar Mantar for the Parliament gherao.The twin issues, on which the opposition is seeking a discussion, are likely to dominate the first week of the session, with BJP in the crosshairs. Congress believes these controversies can hurt the governing party politically as they affect a large segment of Indian population and youth.Kharge and Rahul told Modi that he had announced the formation of Shri Ram Janmbhoomi Teerth Kshetra in Parliament, and its members appointed by Centre are closely associated with RSS, VHP and affiliates, while its "disgraced former general secretary" is "your close associate".They said in the letter,"Your silence now in the face of such a crime is unacceptable."The Congress duo said the findings of the independent probe and the trust's accounts should be made public so that every devotee knows how their offerings have been used.There is a consensus among opposition parties that govt should discuss the issue in Parliament, with SP's Dharmendra Yadav, RSP's NK Premachandran, CPI's Sandosh Kumar stressing the demand in the all-party meeting with govt.Ahead of the session, govt's decision to break up Wangchuk's hunger strike through police action has led to the opposition condemning the Modi govt. -
Article Summary: Mallikarjun kharge and rahul gandhi demand independent probe into ram temple donation issue. Cockroach janta party and activist sonam wangchuk planned to march to parliament monday over paper leaks. Many opposition parties have supported the protest, and are likely to turn up at jantar mantar for the parliament gherao.

Article Title: 'We intend to work with PM Modi': Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar announces entry into NDA with 20 Mamata rebels
Article Text:
Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar (ANI file photo)

NEW DELHI: Once a Mamata loyalist and now an NCPI leader after rebelling against the Mamata Banerjee-led Trinamool Congress , Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar on Su nda y announced that she would join the ruling NDA in the Lok Sabha ahead of Monsoon Session. She said 20 former TMC MPs, who have formed a separate parliamentary group under the NCPI, would also join the PM Modi-led alliance in the Lower House.She said the group intends to work with the NDA under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union home minister Amit Shah.The announcement marks a significant step in the Mamata rebels' realignment amid the continuing exodus from the TMC following its crushing defeat to the BJP in the West Bengal assembly elections in May.Addressing the media, Kakoli said the group had decided to back the NDA and work under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union home minister Amit Shah."We, 20 AITC MPs, are joining the NDA alliance. Leaving AITC, we align ourselves with NCPI... we intend to work with the NDA, supporting the initiatives of PM Modi and our Home Minister Amit Shah and be involved in their work of nation-building. We stated in the all-party meeting that we would work together.There are 8 women among our group of 20, and we will stand by the government on whatever issues they take up," she said.Speaking about the NCPI parliamentary party meeting, Kakoli said the group's focus would be on constructive participation during the Monsoon Session."There will be discussions and debates on 5-6 bills... Everyone should debate, but we shouldn't waste the House's time, as that causes significant loss to the country and the public. In the interest of the nation and for the progress of the people, we will debate the upcoming issues, whether they concern taxes or the registration of births and deaths, but we will ensure the House functions smoothly. That is what we discussed," she added.The development comes amid an unprecedented rebellion within the Trinamool Congress after the party, led by Mamata Banerjee , suffered a stinging defeat to the BJP in the West Bengal assembly polls after 15 years in power.While Ritabrata Banerjee has emerged as the face of the rebellion within the party organisation by staking claim to the leader of the opposition post in the assembly against the wishes of the party leadership. Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar also has led a large section of TMC MPs in the Lok Sabha away from the Mamata Banerjee-led camp. The rebel faction has since aligned itself with the NCPI and is now extending support to the BJP-led NDA in Parliament.
Article Summary: Kakoli ghosh dastidar has led a large section of tmc mps in the lok sabha away from the mamata banerjee-led camp. The rebel faction has since aligned itself with the ncpi and is now extending support to the bjp-led nda in parliament.

Article Title: New bills, old battles, changed equations: What awaits Parliament this Monsoon Session
Article Text:
What's on agenda

Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026

Income-tax (Amendment) Bill, 2026

Supreme Court (Number of Judges) Amendment Bill, 2026

Registration of Births and Deaths (Amendment) Bill, 2026

Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) Bill, 2026

Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Development (Amendment) Bill, 2026

Opposition's playbook

Where Parliament left things

How things changed

Return of the delimitation bill

How numbers stack up

Where NCP, Sena (UBT) and DMK stand

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you support the Women Reservation Bill linked to the Delimitation Bill? Yes, it is essential for women's representation No, it undermines federalism 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

The final hurdle

NEW DELHI: Parliament's Monsoon Session, beginning Monday, could provide the Narendra Modi government a second shot at passing its flagship Delimitation Bill linked to the Women's Reservation Bill after the NDA government suffered its first floor defeat in Parliament since 2014 during the Budget Session earlier this year.Much has changed in the three months since. A bruised opposition has been hit by electoral defeats, defections and the collapse of key alliances, while the NDA has bolstered its numbers - changing the arithmetic ahead of what could be one of the session's biggest legislative battles.The government has listed a packed legislative agenda for the Monsoon Session.The key Bills slated for consideration or introduction include:Among them, the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill is expected to trigger the sharpest political debates.The FCRA amendment seeks to strengthen transparency and accountability in the receipt and utilisation of foreign funds. However, it has emerged as a politically sensitive issue ahead of the Kerala Assembly elections, with opposition parties alleging that the proposed changes could disproportionately impact Christian organisations and NGOs that receive foreign contributions.Besides these, the government is expected to push the Income-tax (Amendment) Bill to replace an Ordinance, legislation to increase the sanctioned strength of Supreme Court judges from 33 to 37, amendments to tighten norms for delayed registration of births and deaths, changes to the Prevention of Insults to National Honour Act, and a Bill to modernise the MSME law by strengthening mechanisms for delayed payments, enforcing arbitral awards and easing business regulations.Meanwhile, the opposition is expected to corner the government on a range of political and governance issues. The first signs of a stormy session emerged on Sunday, when the entire opposition staged a symbolic walkout from an all-party meeting convened ahead of the Monsoon Session. The protest was triggered by the invitation extended to rebel TMC MPs and demands for discussions on the alleged Ram temple donation theft, the NEET paper leak and ethanol blending in petrol.On the temple donation embezzlement row, Congress along with the opposition parties has indicated that they will raise the issue in both Houses, seeking accountability from the government.The Centre, however, is likely to maintain that the matter falls within the jurisdiction of the Uttar Pradesh government.Outside Parliament, pressure on the Centre continues to mount as the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) has called for a march to Parliament on Monday over the alleged NEET irregularities.The protest gathered momentum after activist Sonam Wangchuk was taken to hospital by Delhi Police during his hunger strike, with demonstrators reinforcing their demand for the resignation of education minister Pradhan.The issue is expected to spill over into Parliament, where opposition parties are likely to press the government for answers.In the Budget Session, the government failed to secure the two-thirds majority required to pass the Constitution Amendment Bill.The bill sought to enable fresh delimitation to operationalise the 33 per cent women's reservation quota in Parliament. It proposed amending Article 81 to increase the maximum strength of the Lok Sabha from 550 to 850 members.However, regional parties in the opposition accused the government of undermining federalism by implementing delimitation through the back door.They argued that if Lok Sabha seats were redistributed solely on the basis of the 2011 Census, the southern states' share in the House would shrink, pushing them towards irrelevance in national politics.Prime Minister Narendra Modi, however, asserted that adding 33 per cent more seats for women in the Lok Sabha would not alter the existing proportion of seats allocated to states."The proportion of seats will not change; the increase will be in the same proportion. If you want a guarantee or a promise, I will say so. If there is some good word in Tamil, I will use it. When the intention is good, we do not need to play with words," PM Modi had said in Parliament.Just after the conclusion of the Budget Session, things went downhill for the opposition bloc. Regional constituents of the INDIA bloc had to fight one battle after another.In the assembly elections concluded in May, the Trinamool Congress was uprooted by the BJP in West Bengal, while the DMK lost to the TVK. Also, the Congress replaced the Left government in Kerala.Things did not stop falling apart for these parties there. The poll results triggered an exodus in the TMC as 20 of its 28 MPs rebelled against party supremo Mamata Banerjee and merged with the previously obscure Nationalist Citizens Party of India — now the second-biggest constituent of the National Democratic Alliance in the Lok Sabha.Meanwhile, the "iron-clad" alliance between the Congress and the DMK was shattered after a fractured mandate in the Tamil Nadu polls. Congress extended support to actor Vijay-led TVK after it fell short of the magic number to form the government, leading the DMK to snap ties with the INDIA bloc.To make things even worse, Shiv Sena (UBT) suffered another vertical split after 2022. Six of its nine MPs decided to jump ship and join rival Eknath Shinde's faction.Although the government has not listed a fresh Constitution Amendment Bill on women's reservation and delimitation, it is hoping to succeed where it failed in the Budget Session.With a bruised Opposition reduced to a shadow of what it was three months ago and the NDA's tally strengthened, the government is now considering "suitable adjustments" to the bill to garner support from regional parties across the opposition.The Lok Sabha has an effective strength of 540 members, meaning a Constitutional Amendment Bill requires the support of at least 360 MPs to pass.In April, the bill received 298 votes in favour and 230 against.The NDA currently has the backing of 293 MPs. With the support of 20 NCPI MPs and six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs, the ruling alliance's tally rises to 319.The YSRCP, which has four MPs in the Lok Sabha, has also reiterated its support to the government, taking the tally to 323."Our leader YS Jagan Mohan Reddy has clearly stated that we will support the Women's Reservation Bill and the Delimitation Bill. We have already supported it. If these bills are introduced during this session, we will support them," YSRCP MP PV Midhun Reddy said."As for other bills, we will make a decision only after examining their content. If they are in the interest of our state and its people, we will certainly support them; however, if they are not, we will raise the issue in the House and oppose those bills," he added.Meanwhile, opposition parties such as the NCP (SP), Shiv Sena (UBT) and the DMK have indicated they could support the bill if their concerns are addressed.NCP (SP) working president Supriya Sule said if the Delimitation Bill proposes a uniform 50 per cent increase in Lok Sabha seats across all states, "there would be little reason to oppose it." If Sharad Pawar's party, which has eight MPs, extends support to the Constitutional Amendment Bill, the NDA-backed tally could rise to 331.Similarly, Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Sanjay Raut said the opposition could reconsider its stand if the government's proposed amendments address its concerns. "We will oppose the Delimitation Bill, but if necessary amendments as suggested by us are made, then the opposition can think it over," Raut said. This would add another three MPs, taking the tally to 334.Speculation is also rife that the DMK, with its 22 MPs, could support the government following its messy breakup with Congress.DMK leader Tiruchi Siva said that the party is in favour of the Women's Reservation Bill, but it wants more clarity on the delimitation issue."The DMK is in favour of the Women's Reservation Bill on the current strength in the Lok Sabha, but we want more clarity on the delimitation issue," he told reporters after the meeting.Speaking to The Times of India earlier, a senior DMK functionary said the party was open to negotiations if the government guaranteed that the interests of Tamil Nadu and other southern states would not be compromised by delimitation and clearly spelt out each state's share in advance."The government should explain the concrete share of each state to rule out any possibility of the southern states losing out, and that can be the basis for talks," the functionary said, adding that no formal consultation with the government had taken place yet. If the DMK also backs the bill, the tally would rise to 356.Even if the government manages to secure the support of all the parties currently seen as potential backers, it would still fall around four votes short of the two-thirds majority required.A two-thirds majority is not a fixed number in practical terms. Under parliamentary rules, it is calculated on the basis of members present and voting, not the total strength of the House. This means the threshold falls if some MPs abstain from voting.Against this backdrop, the governenment is hopeful that some non-NDA MPs may also abstain when the bill is put to vote, potentially lowering the number of votes required to secure the necessary two-thirds majority.If reports are to be believed, the BJP is also attempting to encourage cross-voting from the opposition benches.According to reports, the party has also been reaching out to several opposition MPs in the hope of securing support beyond the NDA's formal numbers.Congress, on the other hand, has also been reaching out to various INDIA bloc parties to ascertain the numbers the opposition alliance is left with following the TMC and Shiv Sena (UBT) defections.
Article Summary: Parliament's monsoon session, beginning monday, could provide the narendra modi government a second shot at passing its flagship delimitation bill. The nda government suffered its first floor defeat in parliament since 2014 during the budget session earlier this year.

Article Title: 14 killed, many missing as flash floods, landslides ravage J&K’s Poonch, Rajouri
Article Text:
Poonch: Residents gather in the aftermath of torrential rain-triggered landslides and flash floods, at Dhara Sangla village of Surankote tehsil, in Poonch district, Jammu and Kashmir. At least 12 people were killed and several others missing as rains unleashed landslides and flash floods across the twin border districts of Poonch and Rajouri in the union territory on Sunday, causing widespread damage to public and private infrastructure including roads and houses, officials said. (PTI Photo)

JAMMU: Torrential rain unleashed flash floods and landslides in Pir Panjal region of J&K’s Poonch and Rajouri districts Sunday, killing at least 14 people, leaving several missing and burying homes under walls of water, rock and mud that barrelled down mountain slopes. Officials said the toll could rise as rescuers searched for those trapped beneath debris.CM Omar Abdullah cut short his stay in New Delhi and flew to Jammu to oversee relief operations after heavy rain triggered widespread devastation in the two border districts. Police, Army, SDRF, civil administration and local volunteers fanned out across worst-hit Surankote, Haveli and Mandi in Poonch, while LG Manoj Sinha reviewed rescue efforts and directed immediate relief for affected families.Heavy rain disrupted traffic on strategic Jammu-Srinagar NH-44, prompting authorities to urge commuters to avoid vulnerable stretches. Annual Amarnath Yatra, which began July 3, and pilgrimages to Vaishno Devi shrine in Katra and Shiv Khori in Reasi were temporarily suspended after IMD forecast adverse weather for the next three days.Officials said Lower Murrah in Surankote suffered the heaviest loss, where eight members of a family were swept away.Two bodies had been found by evening while search teams continued to retrieve the remaining six. The dead included Noor Safia, 59; her sons Sajad Ahmed, 16, Haqnawaz Ahmed, 10, and Shahnawaz Ahmed, 10; Khalda Kouser, 25, and her 2-year-old son Sofian; Bano Bi, 60; and Mohammad Akram, 7.Four more died in Sangla village, while a fatality is also reported from nearby Sangalani. Nazia Kouser, 28, died in Noona Bandi, and four injured were shifted to Poonch district hospital.LG Sinha said he had spoken to senior officials and reviewed rescue operations. “Affected families have been moved to safety. All departments are on high alert,” he said. “I urge residents to stay calm and follow official updates. I am deeply saddened by the loss of lives in Rajouri and Poonch.”CM Abdullah expressed grief over the deaths, offered condolences to bereaved families and prayed for those injured. His office said he had remained in constant touch with local MLAs before flying to Jammu to personally monitor the situation.Former CM Ghulam Nabi Azad also condoled the loss of lives and widespread destruction.Rescue teams pulled three boys to safety near Peer Kho temple in Jammu after Tawi swelled suddenly. In Rajouri, Army, police and SDRF rescued two boys stranded in flooded Dhangri riverbank and nine others marooned in Thanamandi.Army’s White Knight Corps said troops “braving challenging flood conditions and swift water currents” rescued 11 civilians, including five children.Authorities sounded a flood alert across Jammu division after water levels surged in Chenab and other rivers. Gates of Salal hydroelectric project in Reasi remained open for a second day during reservoir flushing, sharply increasing discharge into Chenab. Police in neighbouring Ramban also issued an advisory after gates of Baglihar hydroelectric project were opened, sending river levels higher.
Article Summary: Torrential rain unleashed flash floods and landslides in pir panjal region of j&k’s poonch and rajouri districts sunday, killing at least 14 people. Officials said the toll could rise as rescuers searched for those trapped beneath debris. Annual amarnath yatra, which began july 3, was temporarily suspended.

Article Title: NEET cutoff at record high, qualifiers shrink by 1.1 lakh+
Article Text:
Highest Success Rate In EWS; 9% Skipped Retest

Highest Success Rate In EWS; 9% Skipped Retest

NEW DELHI: At 213 marks, NEET-UG recorded its highest ever qualifying cutoff, even as the number of candidates clearing the medical entrance examination fell by 1.15 lakh, a divergence driven by a smaller-appearing pool and a sharp upward shift in the score distribution. Moreover, the total qualified candidates for EWS and OBC for the first time breached 60% of their 'appeared' cohort, and the percentage of unreserved qualifiers now rests below 50%. The qualifying cutoffs for unreserved and EWS candidates rose to 213 from 144 in 2025 - a 69-mark jump. For OBC, SC/ST candidates, the threshold increased by 64 marks, from 113 to 177.The UR/EWS qualifying range moved from 720-162 in 2024 to 686-144 in 2025 and 715-213 this year. For OBC, SC and ST candidates, the lower end moved from 127 in 2024 to 113 last year before surging to 177.Registrations remained virtually unchanged at 22.8 lakh, compared with 22.76 lakh in 2025. But only 19.99 lakh candidates appeared, down from 22.09 lakh - a decline of nearly 2.1 lakh. Attendance consequently dropped from 97.1% to 87.7%. The number qualifying fell from 12.37 lakh to 11.21 lakh, a decline of 9.3%.Yet the success rate among those who appeared remained virtually unchanged at about 56.1%, showing that the fall in qualifiers was largely an absentee story rather than a collapse in performance. Under the category-wise qualifying criteria, UR and EWS candidates must secure at least the 50th percentile, while OBC, SC and ST candidates require the 40th percentile. For candidates with benchmark disabilities, the qualifying requirement is 45th percentile for UR/EWS and 40th percentile for OBC, SC and ST.In marks, the 2026 ranges were 715-213 for UR/EWS, 212-177 for OBC, SC and ST, 212-194 for UR/EWS-PwBD and 193-177 for most reserved-category PwBD candidates. The ST-PwBD range was 191-177. NTA's table shows 9.97 lakh candidates clearing at the UR/EWS threshold, while 81,111 OBC, 29,947 SC and 12,452 ST candidates qualified within the lower score band. These are score-band figures and not the total number qualifying from each social category.Separately, category-wise results show that 61.8% of EWS candidates who appeared qualified, the highest rate, followed by 60.7% of OBC candidates. The qualification rate was 52.4% among SC candidates, 51.2% among general candidates and 49% among ST candidates.
Article Summary: At 213 marks, neet-ug recorded its highest ever qualifying cutoff. Total qualified candidates for ews and obc for the first time breached 60% of their 'appeared' cohort. The qualifying cutoffs for unreserved and ews candidates rose to 213 from 144 in 2025.

Article Title: Bill seeks 3-year jail for disturbing rendition of ‘Vande Mataram’
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Anyone who prevents or disrupts the singing of Vande Mataram, which must now be sung in full at all state events and civil investiture ceremonies, could face up to three years in jail, a fine, or both under a Bill the govt plans to introduce in Parliament’s Monsoon session starting Monday.The Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) Bill, 2026, proposes to amend Section 3 of the relevant Act, which at present deals only with insults to the National Anthem , to add the national song to its ambit.“Whoever, intentionally, prevents the singing of the national anthem or the national song; or causes disturbance to any assembly engaged in such singing, shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both,” states Section 3 of the proposed bill.Where a person has already been convicted, every subsequent offence shall be punishable with a minimum jail term of one year.The statement of objects and reasons for 2026 amendment bill cites the Jan 24, 1950, meeting of the Constituent Assembly, where its president Rajendra Prasad had stated that Vande Mataram, “which played a historic role in the Indian freedom struggle, shall be honoured equally with ‘Jana-Gana-Mana’ and shall have equal status with it”.“Presently, there is no specific legal provision to prevent insults to the singing of ‘Vande Mataram’,” the statement points out while justifying the need for penal provision.
Article Summary: Vande mataram must now be sung in full at all state events and civil investiture ceremonies. Anyone who prevents or disrupts the singing of vande mataram could face up to three years in jail, a fine, or both under a bill the govt plans to introduce in parliament.

Article Title: Farooq Abdullah meets Wangchuk’s wife, wishes him good health
Article Text: SRINAGAR: NC president Farooq Abdullah on Sunday met fasting climate activist Sonam Wangchuk’s wife Gitanjali J. Angmo, pledging support for her husband and the people of Ladakh.“May your husband get better. May he remain healthy and may his struggle be successful,” Farooq told Angmo at Delhi’s Safdarjung Hospital where he and other NC functionaries had gone to enquire about Wangchuk’s health. Angmo thanked Farooq.Farooq said he was not allowed to meet the activist who has been on hunger strike in support of Cockroach Janata Party’s demands for education minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation over NEET paper leaks.
Article Summary: Farooq abdullah met fasting climate activist sonam wangchuk’s wife gitanjali j. angmo. Farooq said he was not allowed to meet the activist who has been on hunger strike in support of cockroach janata party's demands for education minister dharmendra pradhan's resignation.
